intro_zh: 先听完整课文，再把买票时的紧张、sold out 的失望、退票带来的希望、以及 next Wednesday's performance 的无奈结尾读出来。

sentences.text: "The play may begin at any moment," I said.
sentences.focus_zh: may begin 是可能马上开始；at any moment 要读出时间很紧。

sentences.text: "It may have begun already," Susan answered.
sentences.focus_zh: may have begun 是猜测可能已经开始了，语气比上一句更担心。

sentences.text: "May I have two tickets please?" I asked.
sentences.focus_zh: May I have 是礼貌请求；two tickets 要读清楚数量和买票目的。

sentences.text: "I'm sorry, we've sold out," the girl said.
sentences.focus_zh: sold out 是票已经卖完；I'm sorry 要读得礼貌但事实很明确。

sentences.text: "Can I return these two tickets?" he asked.
sentences.focus_zh: return 在这里是退票、把票交回去，不是 come back。

sentences.text: "Could I have those two tickets please?" I asked.
sentences.focus_zh: Could I have 是更柔和的礼貌请求；those two tickets 指刚退回来的两张票。

sentences.text: "Certainly," the girl said, "but they're for next Wednesday's performance. Do you still want them?"
sentences.focus_zh: next Wednesday's performance 是下周三那一场，不是今晚这场；but 后面要读出转折。

sentences.text: "I might as well have them," I said sadly.
sentences.focus_zh: might as well 是无奈接受；sadly 要读出不是开心买到了票。
